verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Petilianus said: "To you the prophet says, ‘Peace, peace; and where is there peace?’"

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: It is you that say this to us, not the prophet.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: We therefore answer you: If you ask where peace is to be found, open your eyes, and see of whom it is said, "He maketh wars to cease in all the world."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: If you ask where peace is to be found, open your eyes to see that city which cannot be hidden, because it is built upon a hill; open your eyes to see the mountain itself, and let Daniel show it to you, growing out of a small stone, and filling the whole earth.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: But when the prophet says to you, "Peace, peace; and where is there peace?" what will you show?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Will you show the party of Donatus, unknown to the countless nations to whom Christ is known?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: It is surely not the city which cannot be hid; and whence is this, except that it is not founded on the mountain?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: "For He is our peace, who hath made both one,"—not Donatus, who has made one into two.
